data.title: Delek US Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:DK) declares quarterly dividend of $0.255 per share, payable on March 9, 2026.
data.description: Delek US Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:DK) based in Brentwood, Tennessee, shared that their Board of Directors sanctioned a quarterly dividend of $0.255 per share. This dividend will be distributed on March 9, 2026, to shareholders recorded on March 2, 2026. Delek US Holdings, Inc. operates in the petroleum refining, logistics, and pipelines sector, boasting diversified downstream energy assets. The company primarily manages refineries in Tyler and Big Spring, Texas.

data.title: Roland DGA launches new UV DTF system with improved media, enhancing ease of use and productivity.
data.description: A new UV Direct-to-Film (DTF) system has been introduced by Roland DGA, a top wide-format digital imaging provider based in Irvine, California, in a recent announcement on February 18, 2026. This system showcases upgraded media, offering enhanced user-friendliness, productivity, and flexibility in comparison to prior UV technologies. This innovation is set to deliver notable improvements in production efficiency and output quality, solidifying Roland DGA's position as a frontrunner in the digital imaging industry.

data.title: Dr. Chi's viral series on infertility and dismissed symptoms among Black women hits 10 million views in 30 days.
data.description: In February 2026, a Houston doctor transformed her personal infertility diary into a widespread discussion, shedding light on medical neglect, discrepancies in maternal health, and the marginalized perspectives of Black women. Dr. Chinyere acted as the catalyst for this dialogue, prompting urgent conversations across the nation. The journal she authored originally served as a chronicle of her struggles with infertility. This initiative became a platform for addressing crucial issues pertaining to healthcare, particularly concerning the dismissal of medical concerns. The overarching themes of racial inequities and the unique challenges faced by Black women in healthcare settings were amplified through Dr. Chinyere's work. The impact of her journal reverberated on a national scale, emphasizing the importance of amplifying the voices that too often go unheard in discussions surrounding maternal health.

data.title: Genicom showcasing high-temp UV sensors at AFPE 2026 in Shanghai, with stable measurement up to 250°C using GaN tech.
data.description: Genicom Co., Ltd., headquartered in Seoul, South Korea, announced on February 18, 2026, the development of GaN-based technology enabling stable UV measurement in extreme environments up to 250°C. This move is aimed at enhancing the company's industrial UV measurement portfolio to cater to the global smart factory markets. The company, led by CEO Jung-hwan Son, is making strides in this field to meet the growing demand for such technologies. This development signifies a significant advancement, providing reliability and accuracy in UV measurements even under harsh conditions.
